```{r load_libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r load_file}
order_df <- read_csv('orders_data_raw.csv')
head(order_df)

country_lookup_df <- read_csv('country_lookup_raw.csv', na = c("", "NULL"))
head(country_lookup_df)
```

```{r check_order_df}
dim(order_df)
names(order_df)
glimpse(order_df)
Hmisc::describe(order_df) # distinct values
```

```{r clean_data}
order_cleaned <- order_df %>% 
  # rename column titles
  rename_with(tolower) %>% 
  # format characters to factors
  mutate_if(is.character, as.factor) %>%
  # clean date format
  mutate(across(c(ends_with("_ts"), created_on),
  ~ as.Date(as.factor(.), format = "%m/%d/%Y"))) %>%
  # clean product names
  mutate(product_name = product_name %>%
         gsub("27in\"\"", "27in", .) %>%
         gsub("4k", "4K", ., ignore.case = TRUE) %>%
         trimws()) %>%
  # remove NA in purchase_ts
  drop_na(purchase_ts) %>%
  # add new columns
  mutate(
    # format values in product_name columns
    product_name = str_to_title(product_name),
    # get purchase month and year
    purchase_year = year(ymd(purchase_ts)),
    purchase_month = month(ymd(purchase_ts)),
    # there are a lot of refund date missing, but they are necessary so mutating them into 'refund or not' columns
    refunded = ifelse(is.na(refund_ts), 0, 1),
    # add 'purchase to delivery days' column
    purchase_delivery_days = as.numeric(difftime(delivery_ts, purchase_ts, units = 'days')),
    # loyalty: 1= yes, 0= no
    is_loyal = ifelse(loyalty_program == 1, 'yes', 'no')
  ) %>% 
  # relocate the newly created columns
  relocate(c(purchase_year, purchase_month), .after = purchase_ts) %>%
  relocate(refunded, .after = refund_ts) %>% 
  relocate(purchase_delivery_days, .after = delivery_ts) 

# find weird date
subset(order_cleaned, refund_ts > as.Date('2024-01-20')) # user id 1571b7e3	87a90f08

# update date
order_cleaned$refund_ts[order_cleaned$refund_ts > Sys.Date()] <- NA

# check if update is accurate
subset(order_cleaned, user_id == '1571b7e3')

head(order_cleaned)
```

```{r check_columns}
lapply(order_cleaned, levels) # find unique values
sapply(order_cleaned, class) # find class

# check values
unique(order_cleaned$purchase_ts)
unique(order_cleaned$product_name)

# find missing values percentage per column
order_cleaned %>% summarize_all(funs(sum(is.na(.)) / length(.) * 100))

# find NA in specific columns
subset(df, is.na(df$purchase_year))
```

```{r join_db}
# clean column titles
country_cleaned <- country_lookup_df %>% rename_with(tolower)

# join 2 tables
df <- order_cleaned %>% left_join(country_cleaned, by='country_code')
head(df)
```

```{r}
# write_csv(df, 'cleaned_df.csv')
```

```{r}
unique(df[c('product_name','product_id')])
```

```{r}
# descriptive stats
summary(df)
```

Cohort analysis to further understand order value distribution by year and loyalty status

```{r}
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(usd_price), !is.na(is_loyal)) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.factor(purchase_year),
    is_loyal = factor(is_loyal,
                      levels = c("yes", "no"),
                      labels = c("loyalty", "non-loyalty"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = usd_price, fill = is_loyal)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("loyalty" = "#B8B8B8",
                               "non-loyalty" = "#F4A582")) +
  labs(
    title = "Order value distribution by year and loyalty status",
    subtitle = "Non-loyalty members show wider AOV spread and a higher 75th percentile in most years",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Order value (USD)",
    fill = "Membership"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "grey40")
  )
```

The rest of the analysis is done in Excel
